source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane/Dropped Object

actions_taken: Floorhands instructed to move out of the way
event.summary: On January 2, 2016, the Diamond Ocean BlackLion experienced a dropped object incident when the Control Composite Service Loop was pulled out of the rig's Top Drive System, allowing it to fall approximately 152 feet to the drill floor. The incident occurred during fishing operations to retrieve a lost Bottom Hole Assembly. A pear-shaped ring on the Ring Assembly became entangled on a bolt protruding from the bumper guard of the derrick. No injuries were reported.
recommendations: Upgrade Ring Assembly to Service Loop Jacket Kit for service loops

hazards.name: Protruding bolt on derrick bumper guard
High wind and sea state conditions

threats.name: Ring Assembly entanglement on derrick bolt
Service loop pulled from Top Drive System
Service loop swaying due to weather conditions

consequences.name: Dropped object - service loop falling 152 feet to drill floor
Potential personnel injury from falling object
Equipment damage to service loop and Top Drive System

controls.name: Ring Assembly on Control Composite Service Loop
controls.barrier_role: Prevent service loop entanglement and swinging
controls.lod_basis: Primary design control to prevent chaffing and tangling

supporting_text: the pear shaped rings were installed to help prevent chaffing and tangling of the service loops and prevent the service loops from swinging independently and becoming entangled
one of the pear shaped rings on the TDS's 'Ring Assembly', located on the Control Composite Service Loop, becoming entangled on a bolt that protruded from the bumper guard

controls.name: Proper installation and securing of Ring Assembly
controls.barrier_role: Ensure rings remain secured and functional
controls.lod_basis: Installation procedure control to maintain barrier effectiveness

supporting_text: the rings were installed onto the service loop during the commissioning process of the drill ship, they were not secured together as intended by the manufacturer

controls.name: Manufacturer documentation and installation procedures
controls.barrier_role: Provide guidance on proper installation and hazards
controls.lod_basis: Information control to support correct installation

supporting_text: there was no documentation available on the rig explaining the proper installation or hazards associated with the Ring Assembly
The Operator's Manual and the Installation Procedures supplied by the manufacturer fail to provide sufficient guidance on the purpose of the rings or how to properly install them

controls.name: Safety Alert notification and upgrade kit implementation
controls.barrier_role: Communicate known hazards and provide upgraded solution
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to address known deficiency through upgrade

supporting_text: a 'Safety Alert' had been issued on February 18, 2013 that provided more clarity on the rings and recommended upgrading the 'Ring Assembly' to a 'Service Loop Jacket Kit'
Because the rig had not yet been commissioned, the ship failed to receive the information and was not aware of the Safety Alert until after the incident

controls.name: Floorhand communication and emergency response
controls.barrier_role: Detect falling object and move personnel to safety
controls.lod_basis: Human intervention to prevent injury consequence

supporting_text: It was at this time that the service hose was noticed being pulled out of the TDS
The Floorhands were quickly instructed to move out of the way, just as the hose fell to the drill floor
After confirming that no one was injured

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
